channel.title: LIBRCHAIN 区块链新闻雷达
channel.description: 每小时自动更新的区块链新闻、博客与 X 热点聚合

item.title: Tether, Nvidia and Amazon Back Humanoid Robotics Firm NEURA in $1.4 Billion Funding Round
item.description: Stablecoin giant Tether led a massive Series C round for German robotics firm NEURA, which will embed crypto payment tools and edge AI.

item.title: The Internet Is Furious at Anthropic After Claude Fable 5 Release
item.description: Token burn, silent censorship, and a mandatory data grab—the biggest Claude release has become Anthropic's messiest.

item.title: UK crypto advocates launch campaign against banks blocking exchange transfers
item.description: Stand With Crypto UK says banks are restricting access to regulated exchanges even as policymakers seek to position the country as a hub for digital asset innovation.

item.title: Pending Bank of Japan rate decision may impact Bitcoin price: Should traders prepare?
item.description: Bitcoin’s average price response to Bank of Japan rate hikes was a 22.5% sell-off. Will BOJ’s upcoming policy decision trigger another BTC price crash?

item.title: AI Helped People Spot Fake News—Then Made Them Worse at It: MIT
item.description: An MIT study found AI assistants improved misinformation detection in the moment, but appeared to weaken users' ability to spot falsehoods on their own.

item.title: Privacy returns to focus as Ethereum developers explore new token standards
item.description: In this week's edition of The Protocol Newsletter, we're looking at where privacy is headed in the Ethereum ecosystem.

item.title: Botanix Will Shut Down Bitcoin Layer-2 Network in July, Citing Lack of DeFi Demand
item.description: Botanix asked users to withdraw funds, disclosing plans to wind down its Bitcoin-based layer-2 network in July.

item.title: BlackRock and Fidelity are quietly turning bitcoin ETFs into a two-firm market
item.description: BlackRock's IBIT and Fidelity's FBTC are attracting the vast majority of new bitcoin ETF money, leaving smaller funds increasingly sidelined as institutional investors consolidate around the industry's largest players.

item.title: Coinbase-backed Stand With Crypto calls on members to campaign against banks blocking digital asset transactions
item.description: Stand With Crypto UK is telling its members to file formal complaints with high-street banks over sector-wide transfer bans.

item.title: Bitcoin Is Getting Closer to the Bottom, But Demand Is Falling: CryptoQuant
item.description: Bitcoin's bear market might be nearing an end, analysts said, but individuals and institutions aren't lining up to buy the top crypto asset.

item.title: Anchorage backs Treasury’s GENIUS AML rules, seeks secondary-market sanctions clarity
item.description: A public comment letter argues that regulated stablecoin issuers need clearer compliance standards to avoid sanctions risks tied to secondary-market activity.

item.title: 7 Factors That Actually Matter When Choosing a Crypto Swap Platform
item.description: With crypto swaps growing in popularity, the best crypto swap platforms are embracing speed, security, and asset availability.

item.title: Mastercard Enables AI Agent Payments With Help From Crypto Giants Like Coinbase, Ripple
item.description: Mastercard's Agent Pay for Machines is designed to let AI buy services and settle transactions using cards, bank accounts, and stablecoins.

item.title: Crypto Long & Short: Who answers the 3am call when DeFi breaks?
item.description: In this week's Crypto Long & Short, Ben Nadareski writes that to win over big investors, DeFi builders must act like accountable money managers, not just software developers. Then, Stephen Stonberg says that bitcoin holders can survive cra…

item.title: Netomi CEO says $5 trillion AI customer experience market could boost stablecoin demand
item.description: Puneet Mehta, a former high-level Wall Street engineer and data scientist, said the rapid expansion of AI enterprise software will increase demand for stablecoins and blockchain.

item.title: The quantum clock is ticking: it's Bitcoin's problem, not Ethereum's
item.description: A recent research note published by Citi analysts reached a conclusion about quantum risk that should give every institutional bitcoin holder pause, Tabar explains.

item.title: New CFTC Rules on Prediction Markets Would Ban Wagers on Ouster of US Enemies
item.description: Proposed CFTC rules would forbid markets where an outcome could be impacted by war or assassination, even when conflict isn’t mentioned.

item.title: Mastercard prepares for a future where AI agents make payments
item.description: The company is working with Coinbase, Stripe and other companies to build trusted payment systems for AI-driven commerce.

item.title: Bitcoin rises despite US inflation hitting 3-year high: Where will BTC price go?
item.description: BTC's rebound shows signs of weakening under a string of technical resistance levels, raising the odds of a dip below $60,000 in June.

item.title: Onchain gambling keeps rolling with $14B quarter despite crypto slump: TRM Labs
item.description: TRM Labs said onchain gambling reached $51 billion in 2025, with repeat users and stablecoin flows helping the sector remain resilient during a broader crypto market pullback.

item.title: AI deepfake election ad in Minnesota raises transparency concerns
item.description: A deepfake campaign attack ad in the US state of Minnesota has raised questions around the ethics of using AI in political ads and the regulations surround AI use.

item.title: Kalshi now requires users to reveal employers as it fights insider trading and market manipulation
item.description: The prediction markets platform introduced new measures on Tuesday that it said will apply to markets it considers are likely to face higher risks of insider trading and abuse.

item.title: Bitcoin, Ethereum Resume Rebound as Inflation Hits 3-Year High
item.description: The reading likely supports restrictive monetary policy.

item.title: Paradigm, Hyperliquid Policy Center Push Back on GENIUS Act Stablecoin AML Rule
item.description: The groups say issuers, DeFi apps, and validators need clearer limits on who is responsible once stablecoins change hands.

item.title: Tenev says Robinhood won underwriter approval as crypto markets front-run mega IPOs
item.description: Robinhood’s new IPO underwriter role comes as SpaceX prepares a record-breaking public offering, with retail brokers and crypto-native derivatives platforms racing to shape price discovery and access.

item.title: Fold Shares Jump 162% After Bitcoin Fintech Sells $45 Million in BTC, Wipes Out Debt
item.description: Publicly traded Fold sold Bitcoin holdings to restructure its balance sheet, fueling a stock surge on Wednesday morning.

item.title: Crypto outflows are sentiment shock, not structural crisis: CoinShares' Butterfill
item.description: CoinShares’ James Butterfill said recent crypto outflows reflect a macro-driven sentiment shock, while other analysts warned Bitcoin’s rebound may remain fragile.

item.title: JUST IN: Jim Cramer says Bitcoin and gold are "bad money" being liquidated for SpaceX's IPO. - x.com
item.description: <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiYkFVX3lxTE5wSkR6a01Mb0ZQakppUm5QTVJKR0RfZXF6UGM0RkZ6bnJLM202R1RRc2hQbkttT290Tmp4LWhzTzNfQnZ4RWE3c3IwOG5sVEVjZUoydXdrbE9hTTRnMFVuX1NR?oc=5" target="_blank">JUST IN: Jim Cramer says Bitcoin a…

item.title: Pyth unveils continuous pricing indexes for US stocks and commodities
item.description: Coinbase, Kraken and dYdX are adopting Pyth's new indexes, which provide continuous pricing for US stocks, gold and oil outside market hours.

item.title: Prediction markets get first U.S. rule proposal as CFTC pursues contract reviews
item.description: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission opened a proposed rule for public comment that sets an approach to determine contracts in the "public interest."

item.title: Michael Saylor gets into public debate over claims that Strategy's latest share sale was dilutive
item.description: Strategy's BTC Yield fell from 13.0% to 12.8% following its latest bitcoin purchase, prompting a debate on X over whether the purchase was dilutive to shareholders.

item.title: Delaware Advances Bill to Ban ‘Predatory’ Bitcoin ATMs
item.description: Lawmakers have advanced legislation that would ban all cryptocurrency kiosks statewide, with physical removal mandated within 90 days.

item.title: Bitcoin and gold--bad money, being liquidated for SpaceX. Apple and Nvidia --good money--being liquidated - x.com
item.description: <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiX0FVX3lxTE0tc3NnS2JYM081NkswX0I1QVhDd05seEM4TVNxT3Q4UTZOdXp6all6MC1TQ1ZQZUE2Mk5xbGVDbWhDdWJWNkdQSFE3dFFvdWJGa19XNnJkSmszVXpYVkNR?oc=5" target="_blank">Bitcoin and gold--bad money, being liq…

item.title: CoinDesk 20 performance update: Index drops 1.4% as all constituents decline
item.description: Bitcoin Cash (BCH) fell 4.1% and NEAR Protocol (NEAR) shed 4.3%, leading the index lower.

item.title: BlackRock just filed a new (and probably final) amendment for their Bitcoin Premium Income ETF $BITA and WE HAVE A FEE: 65bps. Obv higher than $IBIT et al but lower than the two biggest ETFs in 'covered call' category which are 95bp and 99bp. My guess - x.com
item.description: <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiZEFVX3lxTFBESUwwbUNQODVJQnhta2w4dmRxNm1kYTZmRnVIUDkxY1BIal9OR0o1R0VKR0hCWWE4VkVzT01zRU41STBOaWFjMDY1NU1hdVdfY184RWdwek5GMjlnLXo2Qm1YQzE?oc=5" target="_blank">BlackRock just filed a new (and…

item.title: 1) Almost a year ago I stepped back from crypto to focus on multiple personal projects. I traveled, I read, I reflected and I built. Today I am announcing the main one: @TVLCap, which I am proud to have co-founded, and a $5M raise to bring structured products - x.com
item.description: <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiW0FVX3lxTE1lTS16Y0NjRGE0OEkxWkdtNUY5SmFHeldNWDBNSjV2aFdfSERVbEdnY0lNc3h0ZllNdEhQc0F3djkwTEZuOEhka0VWdDNkb2huMno4UDZ6dVFENDg?oc=5" target="_blank">1) Almost a year ago I stepped back from cr…

item.title: Bitcoin price may slide toward $30K as institutions dump 450% of daily BTC supply
item.description: Bitcoin’s institutional support weakened as ETFs and companies dumped almost 2,000 BTC daily and Strategy’s buying lost momentum.

item.title: Solana Sponsors the World Series of Poker, Enabling Crypto Entry Fees and Payouts
item.description: Players can now enter World Series of Poker tournaments with SOL or stablecoins, with Solana branding to be prominent at events.

item.title: Myriad Taps Chainlink to Power FIFA World Cup Prediction Markets
item.description: The integration builds on Myriad’s use of the Chainlink Runtime Environment to power its prediction market infrastructure.

item.title: World Series of Poker adds Solana payments for tournament buy-ins
item.description: The move marks an integration of blockchain-based payments into a major live sporting and gaming event.

item.title: World Series of Poker, Dealt by Solana
item.description: Solana is the official Presenting sponsor of the World Series of Poker 2026, and players can enter into tournaments directly with crypto on Solana with zero processing fees.

item.title: Morning Minute: Crypto Falls Amidst War Escalation and Mythos Release
item.description: Markets sold off ahead of CPI. Crypto tax bills hit a wall in the House. And Morpho closed one of the biggest DeFi rounds in a long time.

item.title: Bitcoin trims losses after core CPI rises less than feared 0.2% in May
item.description: Headline inflation rose an expected 0.5% in May, but the beat on the core rate — which cuts out food and energy costs — is pleasing markets.

item.title: The bitcoin bear market has presented a great opportunity for long term investors to acquire more bitcoin. Don’t be fooled by the headlines. I joined @SquawkCNBC to explain. - x.com
item.description: <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiYEFVX3lxTE96SkdCOThzRWYtNUdzNEx3Rl9nZW9qbGItRjBGeEhTNUMySjFpeU41c2tucktib2dTSzhfeWI5R0tkdlpGU1VvbkxJMGFJU0xvSHMwQnVQdm02WkR3QndFcQ?oc=5" target="_blank">The bitcoin bear market has presente…

item.title: Equipment finance platform Trad.Fi to bring $650M in private credit onchain
item.description: Trad.Fi plans to bring up to $650 million in equipment-finance credit onchain, targeting a trillion-dollar US market still dominated by paperwork.

item.title: A 'Bitcoin DeFi' project just shut down with a brutal post-mortem: Users just didn't care
item.description: "It did not work," Botanix said. "At least not in this market and not in this timeline."

item.title: Botanix to shut down after 4 years, cites weak demand for Bitcoin DeFi
item.description: The Spiderchain developer told users to withdraw assets by July 9 after concluding demand for Bitcoin-native DeFi was not sufficient to support the network.

item.title: ‘Intense Capitulation’ Hits Crypto as 8M BTC, Bulk of ETH Supply Sit at Loss
item.description: Millions of Bitcoin are underwater as experts say the 'scale of market reset' signals capitulation, but long-term opportunities remain.

item.title: The inflation scenario that could send bitcoin tumbling below $60,000
item.description: Your day-ahead look for June 10, 2026

item.title: Kalshi Rolls Out New Safeguards After Insider Trading Concerns Hit Prediction Markets
item.description: Kalshi's new rules force traders to disclose their employers before trading high-risk markets flagged for insider trading or manipulation.

item.title: Zcash, Hyperliquid tokens lead losses as traders bet against a bitcoin bounce
item.description: Crypto markets are under pressure ahead of key U.S. inflation data due later Wednesday.

item.title: EU proposes ban on 11 crypto platforms in Russia sanctions push
item.description: The proposed measures would ban transactions on 11 crypto platforms and expand sanctions targeting networks accused of helping Russia evade restrictions.

item.title: Japan's Largest Banks Plan Joint Stablecoin Launch by March 2027
item.description: Megabanks MUFG Bank, Mizuho Bank and SMBC have formed a council to develop frameworks for jointly issuing a stablecoin in fiscal year 2026.

item.title: Japan's three largest banks aim for joint stablecoin issue by March
item.description: MUFG, SMBC and Mizuho will establish a council to explore operational frameworks and prepare for the issuance of stablecoins.

item.title: XRP market shows signs of capitulation as holders sell at loss
item.description: XRP holders are capitulating, according to data tracked by Glassnode. That suggests a bottom may be near.

item.title: SpaceX IPO nears 4 times oversubscribed, squeezing crypto and tech
item.description: Tech stocks and crypto are selling off in a “classic pre-mega-IPO liquidity squeeze,” say analysts.

item.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX IPO is four times oversubscribed. A crypto bet tells a more cautious story
item.description: The SPCX perpetual still trades above SpaceX’s $135 offer price, but it has fallen sharply from its May highs as traders mark down the first-day premium.

item.title: Chainalysis, South Korean police link up to fight crypto crime
item.description: South Korea's national police has been battling crypto-enabled crimes from DPRK-state level threats to scams targeting retail investors.

item.title: Hyperliquid, Paradigm urge revision of GENIUS money-laundering rule
item.description: The Hyperliquid Policy Center and Paradigm say the Treasury’s money-laundering rules for the GENIUS Act are too onerous for stablecoin issuers.

item.title: 'Maximal' ban on insider trading would hurt prediction markets, says researcher
item.description: “The same insider trade that improves the accuracy of the price today can reduce the participation that makes the price informative tomorrow,” said Balbinder Singh Gill.
